visual_checks: Identify all drag-and-drop interactions on the page — sortable lists, reorderable items, draggable cards, file upload drop zones, slider thumbs, and movable panels
Check whether each dragging interaction has a single-pointer alternative that does not require dragging, such as buttons to move items up/down, click-to-select then click-to-place, or arrow controls
Look at range sliders and verify they can be operated by clicking on the track at the desired position or via increment/decrement buttons, not only by dragging the thumb
Examine Kanban boards, sortable tables, and drag-to-reorder interfaces for alternative controls like move buttons, position dropdowns, or context menus
Check color pickers, crop tools, and resizable panels for non-drag alternatives such as numeric inputs or preset options
pass_conditions: Every drag-and-drop interaction provides a single-pointer, non-dragging alternative such as click-based controls, buttons, or select menus
Sortable lists provide move-up and move-down buttons or a position selector for each item as an alternative to drag reordering
Slider controls can be operated by clicking or tapping on the desired position on the track, not only by dragging the thumb
File upload areas that support drag-and-drop also provide a 'Browse' or 'Choose file' button for single-click selection
Movable or resizable panels provide alternative controls such as position presets, snap-to buttons, or numeric width/height inputs
fail_conditions: A sortable list can only be reordered by dragging items with no buttons, menu, or other single-pointer alternative
A slider can only be operated by dragging the thumb — clicking on the track does nothing and no increment/decrement buttons are provided
A Kanban board requires dragging cards between columns with no alternative click-based workflow to move cards
A file upload zone only accepts drag-and-drop with no browse button or alternative upload method
A color picker requires dragging a selector across a gradient with no text input for color values and no preset swatches
na_conditions: Page contains no drag-and-drop interactions, draggable elements, or slider controls that require a dragging movement
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Drag-and-drop behavior could not be fully evaluated because it requires real pointer interaction beyond what static analysis can detect
common_mistakes: Providing only keyboard alternatives (arrow keys) but no single-pointer non-drag alternative — this criterion requires a pointer-based alternative that does not require dragging
Assuming that HTML5 native drag-and-drop is accessible by default — it still requires a non-drag alternative
Implementing a slider with only a draggable thumb and no click-on-track support or increment/decrement buttons
Overlooking drag interactions in third-party libraries such as sortable.js, react-dnd, or chart editors that lack built-in non-drag alternatives
File upload areas that only accept drag-and-drop without a standard file input button

examples.pass: A task list allows drag-to-reorder but each item also has 'Move up' and 'Move down' buttons that reorder with a single click
examples.fail: A Kanban board requires dragging cards between columns — users who cannot perform a sustained drag gesture have no way to move tasks between statuses
examples.partial: Most drag interactions provide button alternatives, but a resizable sidebar panel can only be resized by dragging its edge with no alternative resize controls

plain_meaning: Any function that uses a dragging movement must also be operable with a single pointer without dragging, unless dragging is essential or the dragging behavior is determined by the user agent and not modified by the author. Users who cannot perform a sustained drag must have a non-drag alternative such as tap-to-select, buttons, or click-to-place.
scope_applies_to: Sliders and range controls operated by dragging
Custom drag interactions for file uploads, sorting, rearranging
Map panning implemented by author scripts
Any author-implemented dragging that performs functionality
scope_does_not_apply_to: Essential dragging where the drag path itself is the function (drawing, signatures, freehand art)
User-agent-implemented dragging the author has not modified (native scrollbars)
Simple click/tap interactions with no drag at all
borderline_calibration: If a non-drag alternative exists (buttons to move items up/down, numeric input for a slider, click-to-place), it passes.
Sliders with keyboard support still need a single-pointer non-drag alternative for this specific SC; keyboard does not count.
Only flag when dragging is genuinely the only pointer way to complete the task.
